qa_pairs.question: What is the process for submitting a batch job on Trillium using the Open Composer app?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit a batch job on Trillium using Open Composer, first select a job template. On the job submission page, configure the job parameters, such as the number of nodes, tasks per node, wall clock time, and output file name. Additionally, set the 'Script Location', 'Script Name', 'Job Name', and choose the 'Cluster' (e.g., Trillium or Trillium-GPU, which provides an option for GPU resources). Once satisfied with the job script and parameters, click the 'Submit' button.

qa_pairs.question: How can users monitor the status and details of their submitted jobs within the Open Composer app?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can monitor their submitted jobs in Open Composer by navigating to the 'History' tab. This tab displays a list of all submitted jobs along with their status, such as Queued, Running, Completed, or Failed. Users can filter jobs using a text box or checkboxes, and clicking on different column fields like 'Job ID', 'Application', 'Script Location', or 'Script Name' provides more detailed information or options, including opening the job in my.SciNet or accessing the script editor.

qa_pairs.question: What configurable fields are available when submitting a job through the Open Composer app, and what do they control?
qa_pairs.answer: When submitting a job through the Open Composer app, configurable fields include 'Script Location', which specifies where the job script will be saved and run from; 'Script Name', which is the name of the job script file; 'Job Name', which appears in the job queue; and 'Cluster', allowing selection between Trillium (default) or Trillium-GPU. Selecting Trillium-GPU provides an additional parameter to request GPU resources. Users also configure job parameters like the number of nodes, tasks per node, wall clock time, and output file name.
